title: quercus appenina var. rumelica
intro: Quercus appenina var. rumelica, also known as Rumelian oak, is a variety of the Quercus appenina tree, native to the Balkan Peninsula. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help with wound healing and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and it's also used to support the immune system. Additionally, Quercus appenina var. rumelica is used in herbal remedies to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related conditions.
